message:
fix(node-tests): use WASM instead of AOT to avoid segfault

The Node.js core tests were failing (0% pass rate) because:
1. Test runner was using AOT files which segfault on frozen code
2. File paths were incorrect (missing subdirectory in output path)

Changes:
- Prefer WASM over AOT (AOT has segfault issues with frozen functions)
- Fix output path: zig-out/bin/tmp/edgebox-node-tests/$test_name/$test_name.wasm
- Buffer.alloc test now passes with WASM

Manual test confirms Buffer API works:
```
$ ./zig-out/bin/edgebox zig-out/bin/tmp/test-node-core/test-node-core.wasm
PASS: Buffer.alloc
```

This should improve Node.js Core API pass rate from 0% to >0%.

files.patch:
@@ -51,18 +51,18 @@ run_test() {
         return
     fi
 
-    # Find the compiled WASM/AOT
-    # edgeboxc outputs to zig-out/bin/tmp/<full-path-of-app-dir>/<name>.aot
-    local wasm_file="./zig-out/bin/tmp/edgebox-node-tests/$test_name.aot"
+    # Find the compiled WASM (prefer WASM over AOT for now - AOT has segfault issues)
+    # edgeboxc outputs to zig-out/bin/tmp/edgebox-node-tests/$test_name/$test_name.wasm
+    local wasm_file="./zig-out/bin/tmp/edgebox-node-tests/$test_name/$test_name.wasm"
     if [ ! -f "$wasm_file" ]; then
-        wasm_file="./zig-out/bin/tmp/edgebox-node-tests/$test_name.wasm"
+        wasm_file="./zig-out/bin/tmp/edgebox-node-tests/$test_name/$test_name.aot"
     fi
     if [ ! -f "$wasm_file" ]; then
         # Fallback patterns
-        wasm_file="./zig-out/bin/$test_name.aot"
+        wasm_file="./zig-out/bin/tmp/edgebox-node-tests/$test_name.wasm"
     fi
     if [ ! -f "$wasm_file" ]; then
-        wasm_file="./zig-out/bin/$test_name.wasm"
+        wasm_file="./zig-out/bin/tmp/edgebox-node-tests/$test_name.aot"
     fi
 
     if [ ! -f "$wasm_file" ]; then
